construction: 12 exact cases across minimal-evidence recurrence inference, 12-job constrained optimization, 17-person nested logic

cases.prompt:
This sequence is generated by a periodic recurrence over modular arithmetic. For n >= 4: a[n] = (p[n mod 4]*a[n-1] + q[n mod 4]*a[n-2] + t*a[n-3] + r*n + s) mod M. The unknown modulus M is exactly one of 97, 101, or 103. The 8 periodic coefficients p[0..3], q[0..3] are each one of -2, -1, 1, or 2. The global coefficient t is also one of those four values. The coefficient r is a nonzero integer from -4 through 4, and s is an integer from -4 through 4. Remainders are reported in the range 0 through M-1. Indexing begins at n=1. Infer and verify the unique system, then return the next 3 terms.

Assign twelve compute jobs to machines W, X, Y, and Z. Each machine must receive exactly three jobs. Minimize total cost while satisfying every interaction. The optimum is unique, but several feasible assignments are close in cost. Return the complete assignment and exact total cost.

Job | W | X | Y | Z
--- | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---:
J1 | 17 | 33 | 35 | 17
J2 | 7 | 6 | 28 | 27
J3 | 8 | 20 | 15 | 4
J4 | 4 | 29 | 5 | 23
J5 | 15 | 8 | 22 | 7
J6 | 6 | 1 | 7 | 4
J7 | 31 | 25 | 29 | 22
J8 | 26 | 24 | 23 | 13
J9 | 30 | 19 | 6 | 1
J10 | 17 | 17 | 33 | 24
J11 | 14 | 8 | 16 | 21
J12 | 35 | 30 | 24 | 26

Constraints:
- J5 and J8 must use different machines.
- J8 and J9 must use different machines.
- J1 and J12 must use different machines.
- J2 and J6 must use the same machine.
- J9 may use only X or Z.
- J11 may use only Y or Z.
- J1 may not use Y.
- J12 may not use W.
- If J6 uses Y, then J3 may not use Z.
- If J8 uses Y, then J5 may not use Z.
- If J10 uses X, then J7 may not use Y.
- If J2 uses Y, then J4 may not use X.
- Exactly one of J12, J8, and J10 must use X.
- Exactly one of J4, J11, and J2 must use Z.

Each person is either truthful or a liar. A truthful person's entire nested statement is true, while a liar's entire statement is false. T(X) means X is truthful. AND, OR, NOT, XOR, IFF, and IMPLIES have their standard Boolean meanings; OR is inclusive. EXACTLY(k; ...) is true when exactly k listed expressions are true. Determine the unique type of every person.

- A says: "IFF(AND(XOR(T(H), T(Q)), T(F)), AND(XOR(T(L), T(D)), T(G)))."
- B says: "OR(XOR(T(I), T(L)), AND(T(K), T(H)))."
- C says: "XOR(OR(AND(T(B), T(F)), AND(T(A), T(M))), IFF(T(D), AND(T(A), T(J))))."
- D says: "AND(OR(EXACTLY(2; T(N); T(C); T(L)), OR(T(B), T(O))), OR(OR(T(H), T(Q)), OR(T(G), T(F))))."
- E says: "XOR(IMPLIES(AND(T(F), T(M)), NOT(T(B))), AND(NOT(T(B)), EXACTLY(2; T(G); T(J); T(N))))."
- F says: "XOR(AND(T(J), T(K)), XOR(T(H), T(N)))."
- G says: "AND(XOR(T(L), T(E)), XOR(T(P), T(M)))."
- H says: "IMPLIES(AND(T(I), T(O)), AND(T(L), T(O)))."
- I says: "IFF(IMPLIES(OR(T(D), T(J)), EXACTLY(2; T(E); T(A); T(G))), AND(XOR(T(F), T(Q)), AND(T(N), T(E))))."
- J says: "IMPLIES(AND(AND(T(F), T(A)), T(N)), IFF(OR(T(G), T(N)), AND(T(B), T(C))))."
- K says: "XOR(EXACTLY(1; T(Q); T(P); T(I)), XOR(T(P), T(E)))."
- L says: "IFF(OR(OR(T(O), T(Q)), OR(T(H), T(C))), IFF(T(N), OR(T(K), T(Q))))."
- M says: "XOR(IMPLIES(NOT(T(N)), XOR(T(G), T(N))), OR(EXACTLY(2; T(P); T(N); T(H)), OR(T(O), T(I))))."
- N says: "XOR(IFF(EXACTLY(1; T(P); T(O); T(D)), XOR(T(E), T(M))), AND(AND(T(I), T(M)), AND(T(J), T(B))))."
- O says: "XOR(IFF(EXACTLY(1; T(F); T(J); T(C)), EXACTLY(2; T(G); T(D); T(E))), OR(T(M), EXACTLY(2; T(K); T(P); T(C))))."
- P says: "AND(OR(NOT(T(F)), EXACTLY(2; T(O); T(A); T(C))), IFF(AND(T(M), T(E)), XOR(T(K), T(B))))."
- Q says: "IFF(EXACTLY(2; T(C); T(O); T(A)), OR(T(C), T(H)))."

Each person is either truthful or a liar. A truthful person's entire nested statement is true, while a liar's entire statement is false. T(X) means X is truthful. AND, OR, NOT, XOR, IFF, and IMPLIES have their standard Boolean meanings; OR is inclusive. EXACTLY(k; ...) is true when exactly k listed expressions are true. Determine the unique type of every person.

- A says: "IMPLIES(IFF(OR(T(H), T(L)), OR(T(E), T(D))), IFF(NOT(T(E)), EXACTLY(2; T(O); T(I); T(M))))."
- B says: "AND(XOR(NOT(T(F)), OR(T(N), T(C))), IFF(T(E), AND(T(C), T(A))))."
- C says: "OR(NOT(T(B)), EXACTLY(2; T(G); T(O); T(H)))."
- D says: "IMPLIES(OR(T(O), T(K)), T(P))."
- E says: "IMPLIES(XOR(AND(T(G), T(F)), NOT(T(H))), IFF(OR(T(A), T(L)), XOR(T(M), T(F))))."
- F says: "IMPLIES(EXACTLY(2; T(G); T(A); T(H)), T(E))."
- G says: "OR(IMPLIES(AND(T(I), T(B)), T(D)), IFF(T(F), XOR(T(M), T(J))))."
- H says: "AND(OR(XOR(T(K), T(O)), OR(T(D), T(K))), OR(AND(T(A), T(N)), XOR(T(D), T(C))))."
- I says: "IMPLIES(AND(T(O), T(O)), IFF(T(Q), NOT(T(B))))."
- J says: "IFF(IMPLIES(T(C), T(I)), IFF(OR(T(C), T(L)), EXACTLY(2; T(G); T(Q); T(N))))."
- K says: "IMPLIES(AND(T(P), T(I)), XOR(OR(T(M), T(I)), T(A)))."
- L says: "AND(OR(OR(T(G), T(B)), EXACTLY(2; T(H); T(M); T(A))), IFF(AND(T(J), T(G)), NOT(T(C))))."
- M says: "AND(EXACTLY(2; T(J); T(I); T(F)), AND(T(P), T(C)))."
- N says: "IMPLIES(OR(OR(T(L), T(J)), OR(T(J), T(C))), XOR(OR(T(C), T(E)), EXACTLY(2; T(E); T(D); T(G))))."
- O says: "XOR(XOR(T(C), T(M)), T(I))."
- P says: "IMPLIES(IFF(AND(T(B), T(G)), EXACTLY(2; T(O); T(J); T(E))), IFF(AND(T(H), T(E)), NOT(T(J))))."
- Q says: "OR(NOT(T(G)), AND(T(M), T(I)))."

Each person is either truthful or a liar. A truthful person's entire nested statement is true, while a liar's entire statement is false. T(X) means X is truthful. AND, OR, NOT, XOR, IFF, and IMPLIES have their standard Boolean meanings; OR is inclusive. EXACTLY(k; ...) is true when exactly k listed expressions are true. Determine the unique type of every person.

- A says: "AND(XOR(T(D), T(L)), T(M))."
- B says: "OR(EXACTLY(1; T(A); T(P); T(E)), NOT(T(M)))."
- C says: "AND(OR(OR(T(H), T(G)), AND(T(H), T(F))), XOR(AND(T(D), T(K)), EXACTLY(1; T(A); T(F); T(N))))."
- D says: "IMPLIES(IFF(XOR(T(J), T(O)), OR(T(E), T(M))), IFF(NOT(T(J)), NOT(T(Q))))."
- E says: "AND(XOR(T(B), T(D)), EXACTLY(2; T(M); T(D); T(G)))."
- F says: "XOR(IFF(XOR(T(N), T(O)), EXACTLY(1; T(Q); T(J); T(A))), OR(AND(T(L), T(J)), OR(T(M), T(L))))."
- G says: "AND(AND(T(K), T(J)), OR(T(C), T(K)))."
- H says: "IFF(IFF(AND(T(M), T(N)), XOR(T(O), T(C))), IMPLIES(AND(T(J), T(O)), EXACTLY(1; T(J); T(G); T(D))))."
- I says: "IFF(IFF(AND(T(L), T(E)), T(Q)), AND(NOT(T(Q)), T(G)))."
- J says: "XOR(XOR(XOR(T(O), T(I)), T(C)), XOR(T(G), NOT(T(D))))."
- K says: "AND(NOT(T(I)), XOR(T(O), T(G)))."
- L says: "OR(IMPLIES(T(G), OR(T(B), T(Q))), IFF(XOR(T(E), T(M)), XOR(T(I), T(M))))."
- M says: "OR(EXACTLY(2; T(F); T(Q); T(P)), AND(T(P), T(D)))."
- N says: "IFF(OR(EXACTLY(2; T(E); T(L); T(F)), T(Q)), AND(EXACTLY(1; T(I); T(C); T(P)), XOR(T(A), T(E))))."
- O says: "AND(AND(OR(T(C), T(A)), NOT(T(B))), IMPLIES(OR(T(M), T(B)), T(Q)))."
- P says: "AND(XOR(T(B), AND(T(E), T(N))), XOR(EXACTLY(1; T(F); T(H); T(Q)), T(I)))."
- Q says: "IFF(XOR(OR(T(G), T(N)), XOR(T(J), T(M))), OR(NOT(T(H)), AND(T(B), T(N))))."

Each person is either truthful or a liar. A truthful person's entire nested statement is true, while a liar's entire statement is false. T(X) means X is truthful. AND, OR, NOT, XOR, IFF, and IMPLIES have their standard Boolean meanings; OR is inclusive. EXACTLY(k; ...) is true when exactly k listed expressions are true. Determine the unique type of every person.

- A says: "AND(IFF(AND(T(F), T(L)), XOR(T(C), T(F))), IMPLIES(AND(T(P), T(M)), XOR(T(N), T(M))))."
- B says: "OR(XOR(OR(T(I), T(G)), OR(T(D), T(C))), XOR(AND(T(A), T(E)), AND(T(G), T(A))))."
- C says: "OR(OR(T(E), NOT(T(B))), IFF(EXACTLY(1; T(O); T(L); T(J)), NOT(T(B))))."
- D says: "IMPLIES(EXACTLY(1; T(K); T(E); T(L)), EXACTLY(2; T(P); T(A); T(M)))."
- E says: "OR(OR(EXACTLY(1; T(C); T(M); T(B)), EXACTLY(1; T(P); T(H); T(I))), AND(EXACTLY(1; T(B); T(G); T(D)), OR(T(M), T(H))))."
- F says: "XOR(NOT(T(G)), OR(T(I), T(C)))."
- G says: "OR(IFF(T(L), T(D)), IMPLIES(EXACTLY(2; T(J); T(M); T(K)), T(F)))."
- H says: "OR(OR(T(E), T(L)), AND(T(L), T(O)))."
- I says: "IMPLIES(IFF(EXACTLY(1; T(J); T(F); T(E)), AND(T(A), T(B))), IFF(T(F), OR(T(G), T(F))))."
- J says: "IFF(OR(T(D), T(P)), NOT(T(O)))."
- K says: "XOR(IFF(OR(T(H), T(I)), OR(T(J), T(P))), IFF(XOR(T(C), T(G)), EXACTLY(1; T(D); T(M); T(B))))."
- L says: "AND(IFF(XOR(T(B), T(I)), NOT(T(I))), XOR(OR(T(J), T(P)), XOR(T(B), T(I))))."
- M says: "IFF(NOT(T(F)), EXACTLY(1; T(F); T(L); T(D)))."
- N says: "XOR(OR(T(Q), AND(T(L), T(G))), AND(OR(T(Q), T(J)), NOT(T(K))))."
- O says: "XOR(IMPLIES(T(C), AND(T(Q), T(P))), XOR(XOR(T(E), T(N)), NOT(T(E))))."
- P says: "IMPLIES(XOR(T(K), EXACTLY(1; T(N); T(O); T(J))), IMPLIES(AND(T(E), T(I)), OR(T(G), T(N))))."
- Q says: "XOR(XOR(T(E), T(C)), EXACTLY(1; T(L); T(N); T(H)))."
